grid_dims_observed.note: Same named grid as EE-03 (columns 1/2/3/4 at 0/4.0/6.0/9.0, rows F/E/D/C/B/A at 13.40/11.40/7.70/5.50/1.80/0.0), cross-validated again here. Unlike EE-03, this sheet has NO trailing dimension south of grid A -- the building envelope on this floor does not extend into the ground-floor-only 'Aprime' dummy-grid zone (matches the master grid file's note that the 2nd-floor beam plan shows only a small overhang there, not a full bay).

warnings: GRID: named grid columns/rows match the master file and EE-03 exactly; this sheet has no southward extension past grid A (unlike EE-03), consistent with the master grid's note that the dummy 'Aprime' row is a ground-floor-only feature.

This is a 2-story house so this sheet only covers the upper floor; the corresponding ground-floor lighting plan is EE-03 (page 61, separate file). Circuit tag '2/LP' confirms this floor is fed from panel LP circuit 2, complementing EE-03's '1/LP' tag for the ground floor.
This house has 4 upstairs bedrooms (นอน 1-4) vs house #4's 3 bedrooms (นอน 1-3) -- consistent with this being a larger house (per the grid master file's floor-area note).

Two distinct small unnamed bathrooms appear on this floor: an ensuite shared between นอน 3/นอน 4 (2 'i' downlights), and a separate small bathroom adjacent to นอน 1 (2 'f' downlights) -- neither carries an explicit room-name label on the drawing itself, room names inferred from adjacency and fixture layout.

Circuit letter 'l' (paired with 'k' in the stairtop switch cluster 'k,l') could not be matched to any downlight fixture on this sheet -- flagged 'circuit_l_fixture_not_located' rather than guessing which fixture it controls. Notably, EE-03 (ground floor, page 61) had a visually similar ambiguous mark near its own staircase top (a dot cluster with a hooked-stroke glyph next to letter 'e') that was flagged there as possibly being letter 'l' -- if so, 'l' may denote a stairwell-adjacent light shared conceptually across floors, but this is speculative and not confirmed.

The small triangular vent-hood icons drawn on the ensuite bathroom's exterior/party wall (top of sheet, near 'หลังคา ค.ส.ล.') resemble roof/exhaust vent grilles rather than a lighting or switch symbol from the EE-01 legend -- excluded from electrical elements and flagged for cross-check against the mechanical ventilation sheets (M-01/M-02, pages 65-66) once read.

The wire-junction-vs-fixture convention (plain filled dot = junction, ⊕ = fixture) continues to be applied here for the 'i' and 'j' doorway dots, following the same reasoning established on EE-03; not independently confirmed against an explicit legend entry.
